data.title: Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months
data.description:
Japan’s trade balance shifted to a deficit of ¥294.24 billion in September, from a surplus of JPY 60.56 billion in the same month a year earlier.

It was the third straight month of a trade gap, coming in worse than the market forecast of a shortfall of ¥237.6 billion, with exports unexpectedly shrinking while imports grew.

Exports from Japan shrank by 1.7% Y/Y to ¥9,038.20 billion in September 2024, reversing a marginally revised 5.5% growth in August and missing market forecasts of a 0.5% rise. It was the first drop since November 2023.

Imports to Japan rose 2.1% Y/Y to ¥9,332.55 billion in September 2024 following a 2.3% growth in August, falling short of market consensus of 3.2%. It was the sixth month of increase but the softest in the sequence.

ETFs: EWJ, JEQ, DXJ, FXY, NKY:IND, USD:JPY, JPY:USD

data.title: Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%
data.description:
• Australia’s seasonally adjusted unemployment rate stood at 4.1% in September 2024, holding steady for the second straight month but falling short of market estimates of 4.2%.
  • The S&P/ASX 200 Index rallied 0.9% to around 8,360 on Thursday, hitting a new record high, with heavyweight bank stocks leading the market higher.
  • Australian shares also tracked gains on Wall Street overnight as strong corporate reports boosted investor confidence.
  • ETFs: (EWA), (FXA), (FLAU).

data.title: Nokia Non-GAAP EPS of €0.06, revenue of €4.34B
data.description:
• Nokia press release (NYSE:NOK): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.06.
  • Revenue of €4.34B (-7.9% Y/Y).
  • Order intake remained strong in Network Infrastructure, while the sales recovery continues to be slower than expected.▪ Comparable gross margin in Q3 increased by 490bps y-o-y to 45.7% (reported increased 500bps to 45.2%), withimprovements across business groups, particularly in Mobile Networks.▪ Q3 comparable operating margin increased 160bps y-o-y to 10.5% (reported up 70bps to 5.7%), mainly due to highergross margin, continued cost control and a benefit from the reversal of loss allowances for certain trade receivables.
  • Nokia's full year 2024 outlook is unchanged. Nokia currently expects comparable operating profit of between EUR 2.3billion and 2.9 billion and free cash flow conversion from comparable operating profit of between 30% and 60%

data.title: China's housing ministry gears up to support struggling property sector
data.description:
China to expand credit support to CNY 4 trillion to a "white list" of housing projects eligible for financing, the Minister of Housing and Urban-Rural Development Ni Hong said on Thursday.

These funds will be used to assist troubled property builders, ease purchase curbs, ensure timely home deliveries, and cut mortgage rates to boost homebuyer confidence. The ministry plans to support a million houses in 'urban village' to be a part of such plans.

Housing Minister Ni Hong noted property sales in first-tier cities in October are already showing full-scale stabilization and that the market has 'bottomed out' after three years of adjustment.

"It can be said that the bottoming out of the property market has begun," Ni commented.

Meanwhile, the National Financial Regulatory Administration viewed that a variety of real estate financing tools should be used to create an 'integrated scale effect, adding that current policies will be extended until the end of 2026.

The new release, sent China's benchmark index SSE Composite (SHCOMP) 0.9% higher on Thursday. The Hang Seng Properties Index was down 1.1%.

The yuan stabilized at 7.12 per dollar as traders cautiously watched the government briefing focused on addressing the country's struggling property sector.

China-based tickers to follow: ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (KWEB), (CQQQ), (MCHI), (ASHR), (YINN), (TDF), (CHIQ), (GXC), (EWH), (KBA), (YANG), (CXSE), (CAF), (CWEB), (PGJ), (KURE).

Currencies (USD:CNY) (CNY:USD)

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Initial Jobless Claims

Initial Jobless Claims measures the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time during the past week. The number of individuals filing for unemployment benefits in the U.S. is expected to decline to 241K from 258K, highest level in 14 months.

8:30 AM Retail Sales

Retail Sales measure the change in the total value of sales at the retail level. It is the foremost indicator of consumer spending, which accounts for the majority of overall economic activity. September retail sales are expcted to rise 0.3% over the prior month after gaining 0.1% in August.

8:30 AM Philly Fed Business Outlook

The Philadelphia Federal Reserve Manufacturing Index rates the relative level of general business conditions in Philadelphia. The index is expected to rise to 3 in October after recovering to 1.7 in September.

9:15 AM Industrial Production

The industrial production index is a monthly economic indicator that measures the real output of a country's manufacturing, mining, electric, and gas industries. Industrial production is expected to decline 0.1% in September after rising 0.8% in August

10:00 AM Housing Market Index

The National Association of Home Builders (NAHB) Housing Market Index (HMI) is a survey of 900 home builders who rate the relative level of current and future single-family home sales. The Housing Market Index is expected to rise for the second month to 43 after gaining to 41 in September.

10:00 AM Business Inventories

Business Inventories measures the change in the worth of unsold goods held by manufacturers, wholesalers, and retailers. Total business inventories are expected to go up by 0.3% in August, following a 0.4% increase in July.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Inventory

The Energy Information Administration Natural Gas Storage report measures the change in the number of cubic feet of natural gas held in underground storage over the prior week. US utilities added 82 billion cubic feet of gas into storage during the week ended October 5, 2024, above market expectations of a 73 bcf rise.

11:00 AM EIA Petroleum Inventories

The Energy Information Administration weekly data reports inventory levels of US crude oil, gasoline and distillates stocks. The figure shows how much oil and products are available in storage. U.S. commercial crude oil inventories increased by 5.81 million barrels in the last week.

11:00 AM Fed’s Goolsbee Speech

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee gives welcome remarks before the Fifth Annual Exploring Career Pathways in Economics and Related Fields Conference.

4:00 PM Treasury International Capital

The Treasury International Capital reporting system is the U.S. government's source of data on capital flows into and out of the United States, excluding direct investment, and the resulting levels of cross-border claims and liabilities.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed balance sheet is a weekly report listing the assets and liabilities of the Federal Reserve System.

Click here to see weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Expedia gains after report Uber explored potential takeover
data.description:
Expedia (NASDAQ:EXPE) jumped 7% in after hours trading after a report that Uber (NYSE:UBER) considered a possible takeover of the company as it tries to diversify its business.

Uber (UBER) talked to advisers in recent months about a bid for Expedia (EXPE) after an acquisition of the travel booking company was broached by a third party, according to a Financial Times report late Wednesday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Uber's (UBER) interest was described as being at a very early stage and no deal may come to fruition, according to the report. No formal approach has been made by the ride-sharing company, and there are no current talks.

The focus of Uber's discussions was the role of Uber CEO Dara Khosrowshahi, who was Expedia’s CEO from 2005 to 2017 and remains a non-executive director, the FT said. Any approach for Expedia would likely be friendly given the CEO's relationship, and he would need to recuse himself from deal talks.

Uber, Expedia and Expedia Chairman Barry Diller declined to comment to the FT.

data.title: Gold grinds to fresh record highs with focus on bond yields, expected rate cuts
data.description:
Gold futures climbed to new all-time highs Wednesday, propelled by weakness in U.S. Treasury yields and expected rate cuts by major central banks, with additional safe-haven support from geopolitical conflicts and Chinese economic worries.

U.S. Treasury yields fell to their lowest in over a week, making gold more attractive as it tends to thrive in a low interest rate environment; the benchmark 10-year yield made a brief foray below 4% before settling down 2 bps at 4.02%.

"Expectations of a 25-basis-point rate cut by the U.S. Federal Reserve in November are solidifying, weaker inflation data in Europe and the U.K. have increased expectations for more aggressive ECB and BoE easing, leading to generally lower yields which have lifted gold," Zaner Metals senior strategist Peter A. Grant said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for October delivery closed +0.4% to an all-time high settlement of $2,674.00/oz, while front-month October silver (XAGUSD:CUR) finished +0.7% to $31.760/oz.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Central banks remain committed buyers of gold to diversify their reserves for financial or strategic reasons, representatives of three central banks told the London Bullion Market Association's annual conference in Miami this week.

Despite the gold rally, representatives of central banks of the Czech Republic, Mongolia and Mexico told the conference that having gold in reserves is still important to them.

The bullion industry predicts gold will rise to $2,917.40/oz by this time next year, ~10% above current levels, according to a survey of delegates at the event.

data.title: Global electricity demand seen doubling by 2050, IEA says in new report
data.description:
Crude oil futures finished little changed Wednesday after posting heavy losses in the previous three sessions, weighed by weaker demand outlooks and reduced risk premium in the Middle East.

Crude oil benchmarks fell more than 4% on Tuesday following reports that Israel would not target oil or nuclear facilities in response to Iran's missile attack two weeks ago.

Also this week, OPEC and the International Energy Agency cut their 2024 global oil demand growth forecasts, with weak demand in China accounting for most of the pessimism; China also failed to reassure investors over the scope of its stimulus plans.

"The recent pullback in crude reflects a somewhat short-sighted view of ongoing geopolitical risks," CIBC Private Wealth senior energy trader Rebecca Babin said.

"While Israel may avoid targeting energy infrastructure for now, the persistent tensions could still provoke an Iranian response, continuing the cycle of uncertainty in the region," Babin said, estimating a risk premium of $3-$5/bbl is currently priced into crude, down from $7-$9 on October 7.

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for November delivery finished -0.2% at $70.39/bbl, and front-month December Brent (CO1:COM) lost $0.03 to $74.22/bbl, the fourth straight decline for both benchmarks.

U.S. natural gas futures fell as concerns about winter demand weighed on sentiment, with the front-month Nymex November contract (NG1:COM) settling -5.2% to $2.367/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Global electricity demand is on track to double by 2050, faster than previously estimated, due mostly to the scale of China's energy transition, according to the International Energy Agency's new World Energy Outlook 2024.

Electricity demand is set to increase at rates equivalent to adding a new Japan to the world each year, with China expected to accelerate its trend after accounting for two-thirds of all new electricity demand over the past 10 years, the IEA said.

The agency raised its electricity demand forecasts by 6% compared to last year, seeing higher demand driven by the buildout of new data centers and the growing use of air conditioning units, in addition to China's growth.

data.title: Calumet surges after securing $1.44B DoE financing for renewable fuels project
data.description:
Calumet (NASDAQ:CLMT) +22.3% post-market Wednesday after saying it was awarded a conditional commitment for a $1.44B loan guarantee from the U.S. Department of Energy Loan Programs Office to fund the construction and expansion of a renewable fuels facility in Montana.

The expansion would position the Montana Renewables unit as one of the world's largest sustainable aviation fuel producers, with production capacity of 300M gallons of SAF and 330M gallons of combined SAF and renewable diesel, Calumet (CLMT) said.

"This investment will allow us to leverage our first-mover advantage and unique renewable hydrogen and pretreatment technologies to transform Montana Renewables into a world scale SAF producer," Calumet (CLMT) CEO Todd Borgmann said.

data.title: Pfizer's Ibrance shows promise in treating appendix cancer: study
data.description:
• Pfizer’s (NYSE:PFE) breast cancer drug Ibrance, also known as palbociclib, showed encouraging results in the treatment of a certain type of appendix cancer, according to a new study.
  • The study showed that palbociclib was able to stabilize tumor growth and reduced blood tumor marker levels in patients with peritoneal mucinous carcinomatosis. The cancer, which is rare, is often resistant to standard chemotherapy.
  • The study was published in the Oct. 16 online edition of the Journal of Clinical Oncology.
  • Researchers said the study also showed Ibrance was able to slow or stop the multiplication of cancer cells. They added that the next step was to test the drug with chemotherapies and targeted drug therapies to see if its effectiveness could be enhanced, according to a statement.
